title: NASA terminates Draper lunar lander mission
summary: NASA and Draper have ended their collaboration on a lunar lander mission due to major delays in spacecraft development. The termination follows prolonged setbacks in the project's timeline.

title: SpaceX’s only problem is finding more space to work with
summary: SpaceX's IPO has made the space industry a mainstream capital markets story, highlighting its vision of interplanetary civilization. The company's primary challenge now is securing more space for its operations.

title: Serbia signs the Artemis Accords
summary: Serbia has signed the Artemis Accords, aligning with the U.S.-led lunar exploration initiative. This move follows Serbia's previous participation in China's lunar base project.

title: SpaceX aborts Starship Flight 13 launch attempt
summary: SpaceX aborted the launch of its Starship Flight 13 due to several engines failing to ignite. This marks another delay in the company's testing of the fully reusable spacecraft.

title: SpaceX launches 21 satellites for military data network
summary: SpaceX successfully launched 21 satellites for the Space Development Agency's military data network. The launch completes half of the planned Tranche 1 Transport Layer constellation.

title: Nature study identifies a new way to spot nuclear weapons in orbit
summary: A study in Nature Astronomy proposes a new satellite design to detect thermonuclear warheads in orbit. The research is partly funded by unspecified sources.

title: Defense Innovation Unit seeks commercial path to deliver electrical power from orbit
summary: The Defense Innovation Unit (DIU) is seeking a commercial approach to deliver electrical power from orbit, aiming to advance this long-studied technology for military use by fiscal 2030.

title: Building the infrastructure for a shifting space economy with Massi Ladovaz
summary: SpinLaunch CEO Massi Ladovaz discusses reimagining spacecraft launch methods and the challenges of building satellite constellations in a shifting space economy.

title: Trump’s pick to lead Space Force defends plan to more than double budget
summary: Lt. Gen. Douglas Schiess, Trump's nominee for the next Space Force chief, defended plans to more than double the Space Force's budget during a Senate Armed Services Committee confirmation hearing. The hearing took place on July 16.

title: Office of Space Commerce makes its case for mission authorization
summary: The Office of Space Commerce advocated for a mission authorization framework to regulate novel space activities, aiming to offer regulatory clarity to companies while minimizing bureaucratic burdens.

title: Remembering 'Ulysses 31', the '80s cartoon that transported 'The Odyssey' to outer space
summary: The news discusses the 1980s cartoon 'Ulysses 31', which reimagined Homer's 'The Odyssey' in outer space, highlighting its iconic theme song. It reflects on the show's cultural impact and nostalgic appeal.

title: These 'metallic' dunes on Mars look like sci-fi. What are they really?
summary: New images from ESA's Mars Express orbiter reveal unusual 'metallic' dunes on Mars that resemble science fiction. These formations are likely shaped by wind and mineral composition.

title: Earth's largest particle accelerator opens new window into the early universe just after the Big Bang: 'A culmination of a decades-long quest'
summary: Earth's largest particle accelerator has opened a new window into the early universe just after the Big Bang. This discovery promises new insights into the evolution of the early universe.

title: Making history: Watch Vikram-1, India's 1st private orbital rocket, launch early on July 18
summary: India's first privately built orbital rocket, Vikram-1 by Skyroot Aerospace, is set to launch on July 18. The historic launch can be watched live.

title: 'The Starry Night' in space: Dark Energy Camera channels a cosmic Van Gogh (video)
summary: The Dark Energy Camera captured a vibrant image of the Corona Australis Molecular Cloud, resembling Van Gogh's 'The Starry Night'. This highlights the artistic beauty of space imagery.

title: Smoke from devastating Canada wildfires visible from space | Space photo of the day for July 17, 2026
summary: Satellites captured images of smoke from Canada's devastating wildfires, which were visible from space. The wildfires caused significant environmental and health impacts.

title: The Perseid meteor shower is here! How to see the best shooting stars this summer
summary: The 2026 Perseid meteor shower is active and will peak alongside a total solar eclipse on August 12-13. Stargazers can observe the best shooting stars during this celestial event.

title: Astronauts just launched on a Soyuz rocket with an energy-drink ad on it, continuing a Russian tradition
summary: Astronauts launched on a Soyuz rocket featuring an energy-drink advertisement, continuing a Russian tradition of space-based ads. This marks another instance of commercial branding reaching space.

title: NASA Pushes New Wing Design to Find Structural Limits
summary: NASA tested a new lightweight, truss-braced wing design (SWEET-15) and found it performed well even beyond intended limits. The 15-foot Structural Wing Experiment suggests promising structural potential for future aircraft.

title: The Growing Crescent of Mars as NASA’s Psyche Mission Approaches
summary: NASA's Psyche mission captured a composite image showing the crescent of Mars growing as the spacecraft approached for a gravity assist in 2026. The sequence depicts Mars at varying distances from the spacecraft.

title: Establishing a VTE Risk Score for Astronauts Algorithm
summary: NASA's Office of the Chief Health and Medical Officer (OCHMO) started a working group in April 2026 to review updated VTE case information and discuss research on mitigating venous thromboembolism (VTE) risks during spaceflight.

title: NASA Awards Facilities Support Services Contract for Ames Research Center
summary: NASA has awarded a contract to Chugach Intelligence Solutions LLC for facilities support services at Ames Research Center. The five-year hybrid contract ensures maintenance and operations for the center's specialized facilities.

title: NASA’s Chandra and IXPE Study Pulsar in Lighthouse Nebula
summary: NASA's Chandra and IXPE observatories have released a composite image studying a pulsar within the Lighthouse Nebula. The image combines X-ray data from both observatories to analyze the neutron star's magnetic field and spin.

title: La NASA invita a los medios de comunicación al lanzamiento del telescopio espacial Roman
summary: NASA has opened media accreditation for the launch of the Nancy Grace Roman Space Telescope, scheduled for no earlier than August 30, 2024. The telescope's launch is planned for 7:20 a.m. Eastern Time.

title: NASA Invites Media to Roman Space Telescope, Crew-13 Launches
summary: NASA has opened media accreditation for the upcoming launch of the Nancy Grace Roman Space Telescope and the SpaceX Crew-13 mission, both scheduled for the coming months. The Roman telescope is set to launch aboard a SpaceX Falcon Heavy rocket from Launch Complex 39A.

title: NASA’s Psyche Mission Images Details of Martian Surface During Flyby
summary: NASA's Psyche mission captured enhanced-color images of Mars' surface during a flyby on May 15, 2026, using its multispectral imager instrument.

title: NASA Welcomes Mauritius as 70th Artemis Accords Signatory
summary: Mauritius has joined the Artemis Accords as the 70th signatory, becoming the seventh African country to commit to responsible space exploration. NASA's Deputy Administrator Matt Anderson welcomed the nation during a virtual signing ceremony in Ébène.

title: A Tide-Fueled Trove of Biodiversity in Guinea-Bissau
summary: The Bijagós archipelago in Guinea-Bissau is a biodiverse region with mudflats, sandy beaches, and mangrove forests supporting migratory shorebirds and sea turtles. This highlights the ecological importance of the area.

title: Starship Flight 13: Booster 20 Aborts During Ignition
summary: Starship Flight 13's Booster 20 aborted during ignition, preventing a launch attempt on July 16 at 5:45 pm CDT. The mission was postponed due to the abort.

title: China’s first recovered booster returns to port as LandSpace aims for first land recovery
summary: China achieved a milestone with the recovery of its first booster, and LandSpace is now aiming for the first land recovery of a rocket stage.

title: Soyuz MS-29 launches new crew to ISS from Baikonur
summary: The Soyuz MS-29 spacecraft successfully launched a new crew to the International Space Station (ISS) from the Baikonur Cosmodrome on July 14. This marks another mission supporting the ongoing operations and research aboard the orbital outpost.

title: Launch Preview: Soyuz to launch crew to ISS, Starship to fly another test flight
summary: Soyuz is set to launch a crew to the International Space Station, while SpaceX's Starship is preparing for its 13th flight test. Seven launches are scheduled globally this week.

title: NASA’s Roman telescope into prelaunch servicing following arrival in Florida
summary: NASA's Nancy Grace Roman Space Telescope has arrived at Kennedy Space Center for prelaunch servicing. This marks a key step toward its upcoming mission.

title: Booster 20 completes record duration Static Fire
summary: Booster 20, a SpaceX rocket, completed a record-duration static fire test using 33 engines. This marks a significant milestone in SpaceX's development efforts.

title: Rocket Lab sees Electron gains, Neutron delays in first half of 2026
summary: Rocket Lab's Electron rocket achieved record performance in the first half of 2026, while the development of its Neutron rocket faced delays.

title: Progress at the Cape: New Launch Pads rise for Stoke, Relativity
summary: New launch pads are being constructed at Cape Canaveral for Stoke Space and Relativity Space as part of the Space Coast's ongoing revitalization. This development marks another step in expanding commercial space launch capabilities.

title: Launch Preview: Long March 10B and Vikram-I debut
summary: Six orbital launches are scheduled internationally this week, including the debut of China's Long March 10B and India's Vikram-I rockets.

title: Vast’s Haven-1 continues to advance through milestones ahead of planned 2027 launch
summary: Vast Space's Haven-1 commercial space station is progressing toward a planned Q1 2027 launch, having achieved key milestones. The station represents a significant step in commercial space station development.

title: TEC Business to Business website
summary: TEC Business to Business website has been mentioned twice in a space industry news context, but no specific details about the content or relevance to the space industry are provided.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: ESA celebrates 50 years with a booklet highlighting 50 key achievements. The publication marks five decades of the agency's contributions to space exploration and technology.

title: Test firing at brand new green chemical propulsion lab
summary: A new green chemical propulsion lab conducted its first test firing. This marks a significant step in developing environmentally friendly propulsion technologies for space missions.

title: Upcoming ESA–Industry events on CM25 implementation
summary: ESA is hosting events to discuss the implementation of decisions from the CM25 Council Meeting at Ministerial Level, focusing on industrial opportunities and thematic priorities. These events aim to foster direct dialogue between ESA and industry stakeholders.

title: One robotic arm to rule them all
summary: A new advanced robotic arm has been developed for space applications, promising to enhance satellite servicing and maintenance. This innovation could significantly reduce costs and extend the lifespan of spacecraft.

title: We’re back: Proba-3 ready for more science
summary: The Proba-3 mission's Coronagraph spacecraft and ASPIICS instrument are fully operational and ready to resume formation flying to create artificial eclipses. This marks a return to routine science operations for the mission.

title: ESA’s Prodex programme brings scientific research to space
summary: ESA's Prodex programme has enabled the successful launch of the 4DSpace-Daedalus mission in Norway. The programme supports research institutes in participating in European space science activities and missions.

title: Stay space chemical compliant in the EU
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) will host a free workshop on June 2 to discuss chemical compliance in the space sector, focusing on REACH regulations. The event will take place at ESA's technical center in the Netherlands, with an online participation option.

title: The young minds space-proofing ESA’s missions
summary: Young researchers are helping ESA test and develop materials to withstand the harsh conditions of space for its missions. The Materials, Environments and Contamination Control Section is driving innovation with fresh perspectives.

title: Beyond Reach Labs Raises $10M Seed, Moves to NYC
summary: Beyond Reach Labs secured a $10 million seed funding round and relocated its headquarters to New York City. The company focuses on addressing size, weight, and power constraints in spaceflight.

title: NASA Selects Starlink for Artemis III Mission
summary: NASA has selected SpaceX's Starlink to provide laser communications for the Artemis III mission. This contract highlights Starlink's expanding role in space exploration and communication.

title: ISU Founder Shares a Vision Beyond Liquidation
summary: ISU cofounder Bob Richards and Dean Emerita Angie Bukley discussed the aftermath of liquidation and future plans for the organization. The interview highlights the vision and next steps following the liquidation process.

title: SWISSto12 Closes $70M Series C
summary: European space company SWISSto12 has raised $70 million in a Series C funding round. The funds will support scaling operations to meet global demand for sovereign in-orbit hardware.

title: TraCSS Looms Large Over Mission Authorization Hearing
summary: A hearing reviewed the Office of Space Commerce's proposed mission authorization framework, questioning its financial feasibility. The discussion highlighted concerns over the office's ability to manage the new initiative.

title: Falcon 9 Sales Freeze Anxiety and Our Launch Ramp Forecast Model for New Rockets
summary: The reliability of SpaceX's Falcon 9 rocket is in question due to a sales freeze, creating uncertainty for hundreds of space companies dependent on its launch services. This could significantly impact the future of satellite businesses and the broader space industry.

title: Exclusive: Loft Orbital Scales Offering with Apex Bus Purchase
summary: Loft Orbital acquired new satellite bus inventory to support larger and more power-intensive customer payloads. The purchase aims to expand their offerings in the space industry.

title: NatSec Space Policy Nominees Face Questions from Senators
summary: Pentagon's nominee for space acquisition testified that legacy military space programs are incompatible with modern acquisition reforms reshaping the Space Force. Senators questioned the nominees on space policy during a hearing.

title: Payload Field Guide: NSSL
summary: The U.S. Space Force expanded its National Security Space Launch (NSSL) program by adding two new launch providers on July 8, increasing the total number of companies under the military's launch acquisition program to seven.

title: Spinifex Space Launches With Australian Test Ranges
summary: Spinifex Space has launched operations offering suborbital launch campaigns, private range access, and test and evaluation infrastructure. The company will utilize Australian test ranges for its services.

title: Sky Perfect JSAT and ispace Partner for Lunar Exploration Ambitions
summary: Sky Perfect JSAT and ispace have signed a Memorandum of Understanding to collaborate on lunar exploration, focusing on technical information exchange and system compatibility studies.

title: HawkEye 360 Details Tactical Direct Downlink in Valiant Shield Exercise
summary: HawkEye 360 demonstrated a tactical direct downlink during the Valiant Shield military exercise, showcasing a machine-to-machine pipeline in collaboration with Lockheed Martin.

title: Starlink Laser Communications Will Support NASA Artemis III
summary: NASA's Artemis III mission will utilize SpaceX's Starlink mini laser terminals for downlinking imagery and video to mission control in Houston. The agreement was announced on Thursday.

title: BQP Secures SpaceWERX Funding for Quantum-Assisted AI for Space Domain Awareness
summary: BQP Federal LLC secured SpaceWERX funding to develop a quantum-assisted AI platform for improving space domain awareness (SDA). This marks the company's first federal contract.

title: Space Force Chief Nominee Schiess Counts Moving Target Indication Among Top Priorities
summary: U.S. Space Force Lt. Gen. Douglas Schiess prioritizes building space-based moving target indication systems and disrupting Chinese military space systems as key deterrence measures. This highlights the Space Force's focus on enhancing space domain awareness and counterspace capabilities.

title: Webinar Replay | Space-Based Data Centers: Inside the Race to Move Compute to Orbit
summary: A webinar discussed the emerging industry of space-based data centers, focusing on the business case, technical challenges, and commercial viability of moving computing infrastructure to orbit. Industry players debated the feasibility and potential of this innovative concept.

title: Keysight and Sateliot to Explore Blockchain Frameworks for 5G NTN Security
summary: Keysight Technologies and Sateliot will collaborate on an ESA program to explore blockchain frameworks for enhancing security in 5G non-terrestrial networks (NTN). The initiative aims to detect anomalies using blockchain technology.

title: Viasat Demonstrates Automotive Voice Call in BMW Platform
summary: Viasat showcased a satellite voice call integrated into the infotainment system of a BMW vehicle during a 5G Automotive Association event. This demonstration highlights advancements in automotive connectivity using satellite technology.

title: AST SpaceMobile Discloses Delays to Launch Campaign, Explores Vertical Integration
summary: AST SpaceMobile has delayed its launch campaign to early 2027 and is exploring vertical integration while raising funds through a share sale.

title: Swissto12 Secures $70M in Series C Round
summary: Swissto12 has secured $70 million in a Series C funding round to expand its manufacturing capabilities. The company reports strong revenue growth and a positive trajectory.
